title: Pharmacy Support Worker
how_to_become: Apply directly for pharmacy support worker jobs in community pharmacies or NHS Scotland Health Boards. All training is provided on the job.

salary.entry_note: Pharmacy support workers in the NHS start at £25,694 before tax.
salary.experienced_note: Higher-level pharmacy support workers can earn up to £33,016 before tax.

description: Pharmacy support workers, also known as pharmacy assistants or dispensing assistants, prepare, label, and dispense medicines safely under the supervision of a pharmacist or pharmacy technician. They manage stock, answer queries, and in hospitals deliver medicines to wards and check expiry dates.
role_overview: As a pharmacy support worker, you'll use stock control and dispensing systems to manage supplies and update records. You'll carry out administrative tasks and act as a first point of contact for many queries.

entry_requirements.overview: A good standard of education is needed. All training is provided on the job.

work_experience: Getting healthcare experience through a work placement or volunteering can help your application.

education_and_training.on_the_job_training: Mandatory Induction Standards for healthcare support workers and a formal qualification such as SVQ in Pharmacy Services at SCQF Level 6, supported by the employer.

career_progression.overview: This role is a good starting point for a pharmacy career.
progression_routes: Pre-registration trainee pharmacy technician — completing the Diploma in Pharmacy Services or Technical Apprenticeship in Pharmacy Services to become a registered pharmacy technician.

chunks.text: To become a pharmacy support worker, apply directly for vacancies in community pharmacies or NHS Scotland Health Boards. All training is provided on the job.
Pharmacy support workers start at £25,694 before tax. Higher-level pharmacy support workers can earn up to £33,016 before tax.

Pharmacy support workers prepare, label, and dispense medicines safely under supervision of a pharmacist or pharmacy technician. They manage stock, answer queries, and in hospitals deliver medicines to wards and check expiry dates.
Training includes Mandatory Induction Standards and a formal qualification such as SVQ in Pharmacy Services at SCQF Level 6, supported by the employer.

Pharmacy support workers can progress to senior pharmacy support worker or, with experience and additional qualifications, apply for a pre-registration trainee pharmacy technician post, completing the Diploma or Technical Apprenticeship in Pharmacy Services.
